heading: Separation for failure of selection for promotion or continuation; time of
label: 2148 Separation for failure of selection for promotion or continuation; time of
text: If, under section 2142, 2143, 2144, 2145, 2150, or 2151 of this title, the discharge or retirement of any officer would be required less than six months following approval of the report of the board which considered but did not select him for promotion or continuation, the discharge or retirement of such officer shall be deferred until the last day of the sixth calendar month after such approval.
notes: Amendments2018—, substituted “section 2142, 2143, 2144, 2145, 2150, or 2151” for “section 282, 283, 284, 285, 289, or 290”.Pub. L. 115–282, § 123(b)(2), renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 115–282, § 112(b)section 287 of this title1972— inserted reference to section 290.Pub. L. 92–451

Effective Date of 1972 AmendmentAmendment by effective , except that continuation boards may not be held until one year thereafter, see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 92–451section 3 of Pub. L. 92–451section 2151 of this titleOct. 2, 1972
